message:
I've spent years being the friend who forgets her keys, misses deadlines, and shows up to things... let's just say *fashionably late*. So when a pipe burst in our basement last month and I actually *had* copies of our insurance docs, photos of everything, and a list of who to call? My spouse looked at me like I'd grown a second head.

Turns out prepping doesn't require you to be a naturally organized person—just willing to be organized *once*, on purpose. We spent a weekend gathering our important papers, taking some photos around the house, and writing down the basics (contacts, passwords, where things are). And honestly? It felt less overwhelming than I expected.

For once, I was the one who had it together. Felt pretty good.

What's one thing you've tackled recently that you've been putting off?
